data.narrative:
Quobly is a seed-stage quantum computing company founded by Maud Vinet, Tristan Meunier, and François Perruchot. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 identifiable comparables: 3 public companies (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private firms including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to Quobly. The quantum computing universe contains only 7 same-sector peers overall, but filtering for seed-stage or adjacent-stage companies with similar valuation characteristics yielded zero datapoints meeting the minimum threshold of three comparables. Applying an all-sector median would introduce substantial methodological distortion given the heterogeneity of quantum architectures, commercialization timelines, and technical risk profiles across the peer set. This constraint reflects the nascent nature of the quantum computing sector and the scarcity of seed-stage public or institutional data.

Without sufficient comparable transactions or valuations at equivalent stages, triangulation around a specific valuation range cannot be supported by the data provided. Any valuation assessment would require either: (1) direct market signals from recent seed financings within quantum computing; (2) application of venture capital method frameworks with explicit technology risk and time-to-revenue assumptions; or (3) expansion beyond same-sector peers to structurally similar deep-tech seed investments. The key uncertainty is whether quantum computing's stage-appropriate valuation multiples align with venture-backed hardware or software comparables from adjacent sectors.
